content.text:
REVIEW-FINDING: 4.5.1–4.5.3 WARN — push entitlement present and both metadata and code promise hourly/unlimited spam notifications (description.txt: "alert you EVERY HOUR ... unlimited notifications"; NotificationScheduler.swift:8 `UNTimeIntervalNotificationTrigger(timeInterval: 3600, repeats: true)`)
Pierre: Apple's 4.5.4 push rules prohibit using notifications for promotional spam not requested by the user, and marketing every-hour "hot deal" pushes are a classic rejection trigger. Your metadata explicitly advertises "unlimited notifications, all day long" and the code hard-codes a repeating hourly promo alert with no user control. Consider making frequency user-configurable, limiting promotional pushes, and softening the metadata claims before submission.
